# scripts/work_tracker.py
# auto-generated explainer · 2026-06-10T10:00:09Z

## Purpose
work_tracker — track + monitor every cron/fleet/agent job in the repo.

Per operator 2026-06-01: "track all the work, monitor all the work and
create the agent to speedup the work"

Aggregates state from:
  jobs/logs/*.log                — cron output (tail + tail-time)
  jobs/fleet/{tasks,results,status}.jsonl — fleet state
  data/eval/bot/audit.jsonl      — bot decision audit
  data/rag/{chunks,embeddings}/  — RAG inventory
  crontab -l                     — scheduled work

Modes:
  python work_tracker.py snapshot      # write data/work_tracker/latest.json
  python work_tracker.py status        # print human summary
  python work_tracker.py speedup       # parallel-run pending cron tasks now
  python work_tracker.py watch         # tail every active log

## Imports
- from `__future__` import annotations
- from `_` import argparse
- from `_` import json
- from `_` import os
- from `_` import subprocess
- from `_` import sys
- from `_` import time
- from `concurrent.futures` import ThreadPoolExecutor
- from `pathlib` import Path
- from `typing` import Any

## Inputs
(no CLI / env inputs — library module or implicit)

## Process
### Functions
- `tail(path, n)`: (no docstring)
- `mtime(path)`: (no docstring)
- `snapshot()`: (no docstring)
- `write_snapshot()`: (no docstring)
- `print_status()`: (no docstring)
- `speedup()`: Run every scheduled job NOW in parallel — no waiting for cron tick.
- `watch()`: Live-print recent activity across all jobs.
- `main()`: (no docstring)

## Outputs
- Writes files (detected 1 patterns)
- Return values from functions above

## Notable inline comments
- 1. Cron logs (recency + tail)
- 2. Fleet status
- 3. RAG inventory
- 4. Bot audit volume
- 5. Scheduled cron entries
